skill.name: open-source-license-check
skill.description: Check if referenced bioinformatics software/code licenses allow commercial use (GPL vs MIT, etc.).
skill.summary: Check if referenced bioinformatics software/code licenses allow commercial use (GPL vs MIT, etc.).
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 48 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The HIGH-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings matched license identifiers (BSD-2, Apache-2.0) in data dictionaries, not actual cryptographic operations. The MEDIUM-severity 'shell backtick execution' findings matched Markdown code block fences in documentation files. 'Path traversal' findings matched security checklist items describing controls, not actual traversal code. The actual Python script (scripts/main.py) performs simple dictionary lookups and file reading with no eval, exec, network calls, or injection vectors. The pre-computed audit confirms a 'Production Ready' grade (85/100) with security PASS on all veto gates. Risk level is LOW due to inherent file read and script execution capabilities, but no confirmed security issues exist.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged license identifiers 'BSD-2' and 'Apache-2.0' in data dictionary entries as weak cryptographic algorithms. These are SPDX license identifiers used as dictionary keys in a lookup table, not cryptographic operations. No actual hashing, encryption, or cryptographic processing occurs in this skill.

low_findings.title: Shell Backtick Execution - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged Markdown code block fences (triple backtick ```) as Ruby shell backtick execution operators. All flagged locations are inside Markdown code blocks showing example commands for documentation purposes. They contain no actual backtick command execution and are not executable code.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged values inside a JSON audit results file as system reconnaissance commands. This file contains pre-computed audit scores, assertion results, and metadata about the skill evaluation process. It is a data file, not executable code or reconnaissance activity.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Sequence - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged security checklist items that mention '../' as path traversal vulnerabilities. Lines 128 and 131 in SKILL.md are part of a security checklist that documents what should be prevented, including items like 'No unauthorized file system access (../)' and 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)'. These are security controls documentation, not actual path traversal code.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged a GitHub repository URL as a hardcoded network endpoint. This is a standard source attribution link pointing to the skill's origin repository at https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills. Such attribution is expected for community-submitted skills and is not evidence of unauthorized data exfiltration.

content.user_title: Check Open Source Licenses for Commercial Use
content.value_statement: Researchers and developers can verify whether bioinformatics software licenses permit commercial use. This skill analyzes licenses such as GPL, MIT, and Apache to help users avoid licensing violations in their projects.
actual_capabilities: Check if specific bioinformatics software licenses allow commercial use
Generate formatted license compatibility reports with risk indicators
Parse Python requirements files and check all listed dependency licenses
Identify copyleft licenses that require derivative source code sharing
Provide license type classification with compliance recommendations
limitations: Only checks licenses from a pre-defined database of common bioinformatics tools
Cannot determine if custom or uncommon licenses permit commercial use
Does not provide legal advice or replace consultation with a qualified attorney
License data may become outdated as software projects change their licensing terms

use_cases.title: Bioinformatics researcher evaluating tool licenses
use_cases.description: A researcher building a commercial analysis pipeline checks whether tools like samtools, bwa, and bedtools can be used without violating license terms. The skill generates a clear report showing which licenses are permissive and which require source disclosure.

use_cases.title: Startup CTO auditing open source dependencies
use_cases.description: A technical lead at a startup scans their Python requirements.txt file to identify copyleft licenses that could require them to open source their proprietary derivative work.

use_cases.title: Compliance officer reviewing software inventory
use_cases.description: A compliance officer generates structured reports showing which software tools have permissive licenses, copyleft restrictions, or unknown license status across their organization.

prompt_templates.title: Quick single software check
prompt_templates.prompt: Check if samtools can be used in a commercial bioinformatics pipeline.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick verification of a single software license before starting a commercial project

prompt_templates.title: Multiple software comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the licenses for samtools, bwa, bedtools, and star. Tell me which ones are safe for commercial use and which require special attention.
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluating multiple tools for inclusion in a commercial analysis workflow

prompt_templates.title: Requirements file audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan my Python requirements.txt file and list all packages with copyleft licenses that could affect my commercial product.
prompt_templates.scenario: Auditing an entire dependency list for licensing risks before product launch

prompt_templates.title: Custom compliance workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a full license compliance check on this bioinformatics project. Identify all dependencies with GPL and AGPL licenses, explain what each license requires for commercial use, and provide specific recommendations for achieving compliance.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive compliance audit with actionable recommendations for a production system

output_examples.input: Check if samtools and bwa can be used commercially.
output: WARNING - bwa (GPL-3.0): Must open source derivative works

output_examples.input: Audit my requirements.txt for license issues.
output: All dependencies use permissive licenses. No copyleft restrictions found.

output_examples.input: What is the difference between MIT and GPL for my startup?
output_examples.output: MIT allows unrestricted use, modification, and distribution including in proprietary commercial software. GPL requires that any distributed derivative work also be open source under GPL. If your startup sells software as a service, GPL may not require source disclosure. If you distribute binaries, GPL requires providing source code to recipients.

best_practices: Always verify license data against official software repositories before making legal or business decisions.
Run the license check before building commercial products that depend on open source bioinformatics tools.
Document all copyleft dependencies and plan for source code disclosure requirements early in development.
anti_patterns: Relying solely on this skill for legal compliance without consulting a legal professional for binding decisions.
Assuming all GPL-licensed software is incompatible with commercial use without understanding the specific copyleft terms.
Using the skill to check software outside the pre-defined database without manually verifying the license status.

faq.question: What licenses does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports MIT, Apache-2.0, BSD, GPL-2.0, GPL-3.0, LGPL-3.0, and AGPL licenses for common bioinformatics tools.

faq.question: Can this skill replace legal advice?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides informational license checks only. Consult a qualified legal professional for binding compliance decisions.

faq.question: How accurate is the license database?
faq.answer: The database covers common bioinformatics tools with accurate license information. License data may change over time. Always verify with official sources.

faq.question: What does copyleft mean for commercial use?
faq.answer: Copyleft licenses like GPL require you to share source code of derivative works. Commercial use is typically allowed if you comply with the license terms.

faq.question: Can this skill check licenses from a requirements.txt file?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the check requirements parameter to scan a Python requirements file and check all listed dependencies.

faq.question: What should I do if a license is unknown?
faq.answer: The skill flags unknown licenses for manual review. Check the software official repository or contact the maintainer for license information.
